purpose: Build a local Wikipedia-backed helper similar to codecompass/rag_helper for offline knowledge retrieval, citations and controlled use inside Ananta.
goal: Ananta can use a local offline Wikipedia knowledge base as a controlled, citeable retrieval source for research, explanations, documentation and domain assistance.
success_criteria: Wikipedia-derived content can be imported locally without cloud dependency.
Content is chunked, indexed and retrievable with titles, sections and source metadata.
Retrieved knowledge is citeable and distinguishable from repo/task/artifact context.
The wiki knowledge path can be enabled as a controlled additional retrieval source in Ananta.
A small curated MVP works first before scaling to larger dumps.

tasks.title: Use-case and scope for offline wiki helper define
acceptance_criteria: Document what the wiki helper is for and what it is not for.
Initial scope is a local knowledge source, not a full internet replacement.
MVP explicitly prefers curated subsets over full Wikipedia ingestion.

tasks.title: Data model for wiki knowledge entries define
acceptance_criteria: A structured schema exists for article, section, chunk text and metadata.
Metadata includes at least source_title, section_title, source_id, language and chunk_id.
Schema supports later citations and provenance tracking.

tasks.title: Local importer for curated wiki content implement
acceptance_criteria: The system can import a small local offline wiki corpus from files.
Importer supports at least one stable input format such as JSON, markdown export or parsed dump subset.
Importer logs rejected or malformed pages cleanly.

tasks.title: Section-aware chunker implement
acceptance_criteria: Article content is split into stable chunks by section/subsection where possible.
Chunking preserves enough structure for later citations.
Chunk size is bounded and compatible with the existing retrieval pipeline.

tasks.title: Local embedding and indexing path implement
acceptance_criteria: Chunks can be embedded locally without cloud requirement.
A vector index is persisted locally.
Index build can be repeated deterministically.

tasks.title: Keyword or BM25 fallback retrieval add
acceptance_criteria: A lexical fallback exists when embeddings are unavailable or weak.
Hybrid retrieval can combine semantic and keyword matches.
Fallback behavior is documented and visible in diagnostics.

tasks.title: Wiki retrieval service implement
acceptance_criteria: Queries can retrieve top matching wiki chunks with scores.
Results include chunk text plus citation metadata.
Service is separate from repo-specific code retrieval.

tasks.title: Citation metadata format for wiki results define
acceptance_criteria: Each result includes a stable citeable source reference.
Citation includes at least article title, section title and local source identifier.
Citation format is reusable in UI, reports and artifacts.

tasks.title: Knowledge source separation in retrieval layer add
acceptance_criteria: Wiki knowledge is distinguishable from repo, artifact and task memory retrieval.
Callers can request wiki-only, repo-only or mixed retrieval.
Mixed retrieval preserves source origin in returned context.

tasks.title: Config surface for offline wiki helper add
acceptance_criteria: AGENT_CONFIG or equivalent supports enabling/disabling the wiki helper.
Config includes local storage path, language, retrieval limits and embedding/index options.
Unsafe or incomplete config fails closed.

tasks.title: Preflight and health diagnostics for wiki helper add
acceptance_criteria: There is a preflight signal showing whether local wiki knowledge is available.
Diagnostics show corpus size, index status and embedding readiness.
Operator can quickly tell why retrieval is unavailable.

tasks.title: API endpoints for wiki indexing and querying add
acceptance_criteria: There is an API to trigger indexing/import of local wiki content.
There is an API to query the wiki helper directly.
Responses include source metadata and traceability.

tasks.title: Read-model visibility for wiki knowledge add
acceptance_criteria: Dashboard or assistant read model can show that wiki knowledge contributed to a result.
Source summaries are visible at summary level.
Admin/operator views retain deeper provenance when needed.

tasks.title: UI controls for enabling wiki knowledge add
acceptance_criteria: Settings UI exposes wiki helper on/off and key limits.
UI can show whether corpus and index are present.
The feature does not require raw JSON editing for normal use.

tasks.title: Integrate wiki helper into research flow
acceptance_criteria: Research tasks can use local wiki knowledge as an additional controlled source.
Results keep wiki citations distinct from external or repo sources.
The integration does not break existing DeerFlow-style research paths.

tasks.title: Integrate wiki helper into documentation and explanation flow
acceptance_criteria: Documentation or explanation tasks can draw on local wiki context.
Generated output can reference local wiki sources cleanly.
Wiki context remains optional and policy-driven.

tasks.title: Optional mixed retrieval policy define
acceptance_criteria: A policy exists for combining repo context, task memory, artifacts and wiki knowledge.
The order and budget of mixed retrieval are explicit.
The policy avoids context bloat and source confusion.

tasks.title: Redaction and provenance review for wiki content add
acceptance_criteria: Wiki helper output goes through the same redaction/provenance rules where needed.
Stored metadata remains safe for non-admin views.
No hidden source blending occurs.

tasks.title: MVP curated corpus build
acceptance_criteria: A small curated offline corpus exists for initial testing.
Corpus is relevant to at least one concrete Ananta demo path.
Import, indexing and retrieval work on this corpus end-to-end.

tasks.title: First golden-path demo for offline wiki helper document
acceptance_criteria: There is one official example showing local wiki retrieval in action.
Example demonstrates query, retrieved context and citeable output.
The demo is reproducible by another developer.

tasks.title: Contract and smoke tests for wiki helper add
acceptance_criteria: Importer, indexer and retriever each have at least basic tests.
A smoke test proves import -> index -> query works.
Returned citations and source metadata are regression-safe.

tasks.title: Performance and storage limits define
acceptance_criteria: MVP documents approximate storage needs and indexing time.
Chunk count and query latency are observable.
Limits for small laptop-friendly setups are documented.

tasks.title: Larger corpus rollout plan prepare
acceptance_criteria: There is a documented path from curated MVP corpus to larger offline corpora.
The plan covers reindexing, storage growth and retrieval quality risks.
No full-scale rollout is attempted before MVP validation.
